name: TC-NFR-005: Delete User Account Performance Test (Soft Delete via PATCH)
description: Verify that the user deactivation endpoint (/api/v1/users/{user_id}) meets performance requirements when processing concurrent PATCH requests that soft-delete user accounts by updating their status to 'deactivated'.
corresponding_NFR: NFR-5: Delete User Account (Write/Delete Operation) — max response time ≤ 500 ms, success rate ≥ 98%, 50 concurrent deactivations supported, deactivated users must not appear in /api/v1/users/active.
preconditions: Test users must exist in the database and be in 'active' status prior to test execution.
A valid authorization token must be included in the Authorization header.
Test users must not have any pending transactions that would prevent deactivation.

input_data.operation_type: Soft delete — status field update only

notes: Each virtual user must target a distinct user_id to prevent conflicts. After each PATCH, issue a GET to /api/v1/users/active to confirm the deactivated user no longer appears (data integrity check). Pre-seed at least 50 active test users with no pending transactions before the run. Monitor for any partial update failures or race conditions under concurrent load.
